name: controller
summary: Hardware controller init and per-frame read. controller_init (called from boot.c) creates SI event queue, initializes controller via osContInit, detects connected controllers. controller_read_frame (called from buttons.c:button_update_frame) polls SI message queue for async read completion, calls osContGetReadData, triggers next async read.

functions.name: controller_init
functions.summary: Controller initialization: creates temporary SI message queue, calls osContInit. Then creates permanent SI message queue (sSiMessageQueue), sets up gContPadPtrs with sNullPad fallback. Detects connected controllers (up to 2 players), connects them to sContPad entries, starts async read via osContStartReadData. Called from boot.c:main_thread_func.

functions.name: controller_read_frame
functions.summary: Per-frame controller read: polls sSiMessageQueue with OS_MESG_NOBLOCK. If SI data ready (osRecvMesg returns != -1), calls osContGetReadData into sContPad and triggers next async read via osContStartReadData. Called from buttons.c:button_update_frame.

globals.sNullPad: Default zeroed OSContPad — used as placeholder for disconnected controllers
globals.sControllerCount: Number of successfully connected controllers detected
globals.sSiMessageQueue: SI (Serial Interface) event message queue for controller read completion
globals.sContStatus[MAXCONTROLLERS]: Controller status array filled by osContInit
globals.sContPad[MAXCONTROLLERS]: Controller pad data array filled by osContGetReadData
globals.gContPadPtrs[MAXCONTROLLERS]: Global pointers to current pad data — points to sContPad[i] if connected, else sNullPad. Used by buttons.c:button_process.
